question: After editing `orderValue` in Operate from `"99"` to `99` and saving the change, what final step does the guide require to actually move the instance past the XOR gateway?

options.text: Initiate a retry of the process instance from the top right corner of the page or below Operations.
Republish the `payment-received` message so the gateway re-evaluates.
Save the variable change; Operate re-evaluates the gateway on its next polling cycle.
Restart the Zeebe broker so the blocked job is unlocked and re-activated.

a.text: The guide states the last step is to initiate a retry of the process instance, and notes two places where this can be done: in the top right corner of the page and below Operations.

b.text: The message catch event was already satisfied earlier; re-publishing the message is not what re-evaluates the failing gateway.

c.text: Operate does not retry failed evaluations automatically on a polling cycle; the operator must trigger the retry.

d.text: Restarting the broker is not part of the resolution flow described in the guide.

explanation: Per the guide, editing the variable alone is not enough — the operator must initiate a retry of the process instance to re-evaluate the failing element. The retry control is available in the top right of the instance page and below the Operations panel.
